item.title: Replimune - FDA Rejection (RP1)
item.description:
• Summary: Replimune faces a second FDA rejection for RP1, an oncolytic virus therapy, citing concerns that put the company on a challenging regulatory path. The decision highlights hurdles in advancing novel cancer immunotherapies.
  • MoAs: RP1 is an oncolytic herpes simplex virus type 1 engineered to express GM-CSF and an anti-CTLA-4 antibody, enhancing immune activation against tumors.
  • Sources: ApexOnco, BioSpace

item.title: FDA Regulatory & Policy
item.description:
• Summary: The FDA is urging clinical trial sponsors to report study results to enhance transparency, narrowing its search for a new biologics and vaccines leader, and navigating evolving regulations with associated growing pains. The FDA's Complete Response Letter (CRL) transparency policy is also noted to boost biopharma accountability.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Fierce Biotech, Endpoints News, BioSpace, BioSpace

item.title: Medicare & CMS Policy
item.description:
• Summary: CMS is launching a chronic care pilot program to improve access for Medicare participants, and there are developments in Medicare Advantage policies, including discussions on medical underwriting under the Trump administration.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: STAT News, STAT News

item.title: Supply Chain & USP
item.description:
• Summary: The U.S. Pharmacopeia (USP) has added Tamiflu (oseltamivir) and Trulicity (dulaglutide) to its list of vulnerable medicines due to supply chain concerns, with upstream analysis reshaping risk assessments for these critical drugs.
  • MoAs: Tamiflu: Neuraminidase inhibitor for influenza. Trulicity: GLP-1 receptor agonist for type 2 diabetes.
  • Sources: BioSpace

item.title: White House Policy Impact
item.description:
• Summary: White House policies are raising concerns for small biotech companies, as highlighted by the CEO of Rhythm, who describes them as "incredibly worrisome" for innovation and growth in the sector.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Endpoints News

item.title: Sponsored Content
item.description:
• Summary: Sponsored content explores strategies for rethinking dermatology trial design to achieve late-stage success and discusses how connected medical devices are enabling smarter, more personalized healthcare delivery.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: BioPharma Dive, BioPharma Dive

item.title: FDA Leadership
item.description:
• Summary: There is ongoing coverage regarding the replacement for Vinay Prasad in the FDA's Center for Biologics Evaluation and Research (CBER), highlighting leadership changes within the regulatory agency.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: STAT News
